primary_goal: Govern tree-first markdown document architecture inside a skill folder starting at the entry handoff, with anchors, metadata contracts, fewshot examples, and a TypeScript CLI.

role_rules.entry_node: Treat the entry node as the first handoff into the document tree.
role_rules.routing_doc: Carry one branching axis only and route readers into the next narrower branch.
role_rules.topic_atom: Carry one stable topic only and keep local rules self-contained.
role_rules.index_doc: List or route documents without becoming the main rule body.
role_rules.example_doc: Show a real tree arrangement that can be reused as fewshot.

split_triggers: one document mixes multiple independent semantic axes
one document mixes read and write paths
one document mixes multiple languages, platforms, or environments
the reader cannot tell which child doc to read next
stop_split_conditions: the document carries one stable topic
the reader no longer needs a major branch decision inside the document
further splitting would create fragments without independent semantic value
routing_principles: always enter through a stable entry node before deep rules
separate rules, fewshot examples, and metadata into different top-level tracks
prefer branch docs before dumping many leaf docs
use graph cross-links only after the primary tree path is clear
